subject: Science
chapter: The Invisible Living World: Beyond Our Naked Eye
source_pdf: 2.The Invisible Living World Beyond Our Naked Eye.pdf

key_points: Microorganisms are living organisms that are too small to be seen with the naked eye
The invention of the microscope helped scientists observe cells and microorganisms
All living organisms are made up of cells which perform important life processes
Some microorganisms are useful in food production, agriculture, and medicine
Microorganisms also play an important role in maintaining ecological balance
examples: Bacteria such as Lactobacillus help in converting milk into curd
Yeast is used in baking to make bread soft and fluffy
Rhizobium bacteria live in the roots of leguminous plants and improve soil fertility
Microalgae in oceans produce a large amount of oxygen
Certain microorganisms help decompose dead plants and animals
processes: Fermentation: Microorganisms like yeast convert sugars into alcohol or carbon dioxide in the absence of oxygen
Nitrogen fixation: Bacteria convert atmospheric nitrogen into forms that plants can absorb and use
Decomposition: Microorganisms break down dead organic matter and recycle nutrients in the soil
Photosynthesis in microalgae: Microalgae use sunlight, water, and carbon dioxide to produce food and oxygen
exam_tips: Understand how microscopes helped scientists discover microorganisms
Remember the functions of different specialized cells in living organisms
Learn examples of useful microorganisms in everyday life
Explain how microorganisms help maintain nutrient cycles in nature
